skill.name: receiving-code-review
skill.description: Guides AI coding agents through code review feedback with verification, clarification, technical pushback, and careful implementation order.
skill.summary: Evaluate code review feedback before changing code.
skill.author: DYAI2025

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown code fences as Ruby shell backticks, but these are instructional examples, not executable code. The weak cryptography and network reconnaissance findings were false positives; no prompt injection, exfiltration, or executable malware behavior was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences
low_findings.description: The external command findings correspond to Markdown fenced blocks used to show review workflows and example responses. SKILL.md contains no script file, Ruby execution, or shell backtick command execution at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are literal Markdown fence delimiters and natural-language examples. No executable interpreter context or command substitution syntax is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings at the description and example-response lines do not contain cryptographic APIs, hashes, ciphers, or password handling. They are plain prose about technical review behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no weak algorithm names or cryptographic operations on the cited lines. The static signal appears to be a text-pattern collision.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The network reconnaissance finding points to guidance about using technical reasoning and asking questions. No network scanning, external host discovery, or reconnaissance command appears at the cited line.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is non-executable prose and contains no network tool invocation. Nearby content also remains review-process guidance.

content.value_statement: Code review feedback can be unclear, incomplete, or wrong for the current codebase. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code verify suggestions before changing code.
actual_capabilities: Guides review feedback triage before implementation.
Prompts clarification when review items are unclear.
Encourages verification against codebase behavior and tests.
Defines when to push back with technical reasoning.
Prioritizes multi-item feedback by risk and complexity.
limitations: Does not inspect code or run tests by itself.
Does not replace human architectural decisions.
Does not provide language-specific review rules.
Requires the user to provide the review feedback and code context.

use_cases.title: Review Triage for Solo Developers
use_cases.description: Sort incoming review comments into clear fixes, unclear items, and suggestions that need verification.

use_cases.title: AI Pair Programming Feedback
use_cases.description: Help an AI coding assistant respond to reviewer comments without blind agreement or premature edits.

use_cases.title: Team Review Follow-Up
use_cases.description: Prepare concise technical responses when external feedback conflicts with product scope or existing constraints.

prompt_templates.title: Triage Review Comments
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these code review comments. Separate clear actions, unclear items, and suggestions that need verification before implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Clarify Ambiguous Feedback
prompt_templates.prompt: I received these review items and some are unclear. Identify what needs clarification before any code changes are made.

prompt_templates.title: Verify a Technical Suggestion
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this review suggestion against the codebase context I provide. Tell me whether it is correct, risky, or missing context.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Review Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an implementation order for these review comments. Prioritize blocking issues, simple fixes, complex changes, and tests.

output_examples.input: A reviewer says to remove a legacy fallback, but the supported platform range is unclear.
output_examples.output: The response should verify platform support first, explain the compatibility risk, and ask whether to keep the fallback or drop legacy support.

output_examples.input: A reviewer asks for a larger metrics system on an endpoint that may be unused.
output_examples.output: The response should check actual usage before implementation and ask whether the unused endpoint should be removed instead.

output_examples.input: A review includes six items, but two are ambiguous.
output_examples.output: The response should pause implementation, list the understood items, and request clarification on the ambiguous items.

best_practices: Verify review suggestions against the current codebase before editing.
Ask direct clarification questions before handling unclear feedback.
Test each implemented review item before moving to the next.
anti_patterns: Implementing all feedback without checking whether it is correct.
Using performative agreement instead of technical acknowledgment.
Batching unrelated review fixes without focused verification.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps AI coding agents evaluate code review feedback before making changes.

faq.question: Does it automatically apply review comments?
faq.answer: No. It provides a decision workflow for clarification, verification, response, and implementation order.

faq.question: Can it help push back on incorrect feedback?
faq.answer: Yes. It encourages concise technical reasoning when feedback is wrong or missing context.

faq.question: Is this only for external reviewers?
faq.answer: No. It covers feedback from a human partner and external reviewers, with different trust assumptions.

faq.question: Does it include executable code?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a Markdown guidance document with examples and process rules.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: It is marked compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
